task.name: Goal generation from discovery outputs
task.objective: Read validated discovery outputs from docs/architecture/*, generate a precise sequence of taskvisor goal ROADMAP SKELETONS (Tier 1 of the two-tier director flow, docs/architecture/director-two-tier-design.md §5). Each emitted goal carries ONLY id + description + phase + depends_on + a coarse deliverable_area, with status=roadmap — NO validate/acceptance/scope/goal.md and NO per-goal [CODE-RULE-INJECTION]. Those concrete fields are authored just-in-time, against the REAL tree, by /tmux:elaborate at dispatch (§6) — never predicted here at the moment of least information. Goals are atomic and 1-indexed (goal-001, goal-002, …) with explicit dependencies. This skill starts with a validation gate (Step 0) that checks cross-file consistency before any goal generation begins, and after enumeration runs the Tier-1 DAG analyses that operate fine on skeletons: depinfer (produce/consume depends_on keyed on deliverable_area), G-04 phase-ordering, and the over-serialization plan-audit.

input.note: No $ARGUMENTS — reads docs/architecture/* files implicitly from the working directory. These files are produced by /tmux:task-plan-discover.

output.primary: goals.yaml with all generated goal ROADMAP SKELETONS — each goal has ID, description, phase, depends_on, a coarse deliverable_area, and status=roadmap. Skeletons carry NO validate/acceptance/scope and NO goal.md body — the roadmap is the DAG and nothing concrete, so it has nothing that can rot (design §5).
output.secondary: NO per-goal goal.md is authored here. The full goal.md body (Description, Dependencies, Deliverables, Acceptance Criteria, Test Environment reference, Context, Not In Scope, Investigation Config, Validation Rules) is authored at dispatch by /tmux:elaborate (Tier 2, §6), reading the real worktree tree instead of predicting it.

glossary.term: Detected primary language slug (e.g. "php", "go", "python", "node"). Inherited from discovery session state, confirmed in docs/architecture/product-brief.md or project manifest.
Detected framework (e.g. "symfony", "laravel", "express", "gin"). Inherited from discovery session state, confirmed in docs/architecture/product-brief.md or project manifest.
Path to language-specific templates. Resolved via two-tier lookup: templates/<LANG>-<FRAMEWORK>/ first, then templates/<LANG>/, then templates/_base/. Inherited from discovery.
List of bounded context names extracted from docs/architecture/bounded-contexts.md level-3 headings under "Bounded Context Inventory". Built in Step 0.
Map of BC name to list of aggregate names. Extracted from bounded-contexts.md "Aggregates:" fields and cross-checked with domain-model.md headings. Built in Step 0.
Map of BC name to list of API endpoints. Extracted from docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md "Bounded Context" table cells. Built in Step 0.

Total number of goals to generate, calculated from the goal count formula: endpoints + auth_flows + listeners + cross_cutting + decisions + (aggregates * 2) + infrastructure. Computed in Step 1 (goal generation).

llm.mandate: ROADMAP-ONLY EMISSION (two-tier, design §5): every ENUMERATED feature/cross-cutting/validation/final-gate goal (Steps 3.14–3.30) is emitted as a SKELETON — its goal-create passes status=roadmap + description + phase + depends_on + a coarse deliverable_area, and OMITS validate/acceptance/scope/goal.md AND the per-goal [CODE-RULE-INJECTION]. NEVER author a concrete validate/acceptance/scope field or a goal.md body for these, and NEVER run `tmux-cli rules match` per goal — those are Tier-2, authored by /tmux:elaborate at dispatch against the real tree (§6). Emitting them re-introduces the authored-too-early rot the redesign removes (§1). BOOTSTRAP CARVE-OUT: the two bootstrap goals — goal-001 (Gate 0, Step 1) and goal-002 (Scaffold, Step 2) — run BEFORE any code exists; scaffold CREATES the tree and Gate 0 checks the environment, so there is no predecessor output for Tier-2 elaboration to read against. They remain born-pending fully-specced (the daemon tolerates fully-specced pending goals per §10) and are NOT roadmap skeletons. Every goal from Step 3.14 onward IS a roadmap skeleton.
NEVER generate goals if Step 0 validation fails — abort with actionable diagnostics
ALWAYS read discovery data from docs/architecture/* files on disk, never from conversation context (G-16)
NEVER modify discovery output files — generation is read-only on discovery outputs, write-only on goals
ALWAYS validate ALL consistency rules before reporting errors — collect every error, never stop at the first

deliverable_area on EVERY skeleton MUST be a COARSE deliverable path in the resolved topology (e.g. `projects/api/src/Http/ErrorHandling/` or `contexts/{BC}/src/Application/`) — the seed depinfer keys produce/consume overlap on, and the seed /tmux:elaborate reads to find the real tree. NEVER author invented per-file paths or concrete validate/acceptance here; the coarse area is all the roadmap commits to.

conventions.resolve: BINDING CONVENTIONS ARE EXTERNALIZED INTO THE RULES CATALOGUE — load them NOW, before executing any step. Run via Bash: `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=convention --lang=<LANG> --framework=<FRAMEWORK>` (substitute the discovery-state values; omit a flag only when that value is genuinely unknown). The command prints one rule-file path per line, resolved from .tmux-cli/rules/ for THIS project's stack and capabilities (always _base; docker/database/frontend/frontend-auth packs when their signals hold; plus any project-local rules under .tmux-cli/rules/local/). Read EVERY printed file. Each <rule> element — and the <scope-derivation> and <validate-acceptance-mandate> elements — inside those files is a BINDING convention on every later step, exactly as if it were inlined in this block. The rule ids referenced throughout this flow (CMD-CONV, HTTP-CONV, NODE-TOOL-CONV, ENSURE-STACK-CONV, HTTP-WAIT-CONV, E2E-ARTIFACT-CONV, DOCKER-RUNTIME-FRONTLOAD, E2E-ENV-CONV, E2E-SIDEFX-CONV, E2E-DATA-ISOLATION-CONV, E2E-AUTH-STATE-CONV) resolve to these loaded rules.
conventions.topology-binding: RESOLVED CONVENTIONS DEFINE THE EMISSION TOPOLOGY. When the resolved convention set declares a multi-package / DDD-MONOREPO topology — as the php-symfony pack does via monorepo-layout.md + context-layers.md + shared-kernel.md + app-layer.md — then EVERY emitted deliverable path, acceptance-string path, scope glob, fan-out <owns> hint, and investigator file path MUST target that monorepo topology: a bounded context is a LIBRARY at contexts/<bc>/src/{Domain,Application,Bundle} (Bundle is the infrastructure layer — there is NO Infrastructure/ folder) with its framework layer at contexts/<bc>/app; the shared kernel is the literal contexts/previo context (published language + contracts); a deployable is projects/<app>/src; shared libraries are packages/<pkg>; the root composer.json wires path repositories and deptrac enforces BOTH layer direction AND package boundaries. NEVER emit a retired flat src/<BC>/{Domain,Application,Infrastructure} or src/Share/ deliverable path — the flat skeleton is not a valid emission target. step-1-gate0.xml substep 1.2b gates this at plan time (it aborts if the conventions still resolve the flat skeleton). Controllers are single-sourced through RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH (step 3.17.0). Frontend deliverables (Playwright/E2E) gate on FRONTEND_MODE == vue, never the binary HAS_FRONTEND.
conventions.resolve-code-rules: ALSO run `tmux-cli rules resolve --kind=code-rules --lang=<LANG> --framework=<FRAMEWORK>`. The returned files are the project's CODE RULES — implementation/review conventions with severity must|should and applies_to path globs (schema: .tmux-cli/rules/SCHEMA.md). Two-tier relocation: the roadmap generator does NOT attach code-rule pointers or structured fields to skeletons (it knows only a coarse deliverable_area, not real deliverables). Code-rule matching against REAL deliverable paths and the per-goal "Code rules: …" pointer + structured acceptance/validate/investigator injection all happen at dispatch in /tmux:elaborate via [CODE-RULE-INJECTION]. This block stays as the resolved-catalogue load the topology/deliverable_area derivation reads; `must` rules cannot be waived by implementers.
conventions.rule-to-goal-injection:
RELOCATION NOTE (two-tier, design §5/§6): under roadmap-only emission this generator's shards NO LONGER invoke `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` per goal — they emit skeletons with no validate/acceptance/investigation_config to inject into. The mechanic is now INVOKED at dispatch by /tmux:elaborate (its step 6) against the REAL deliverable paths. The DEFINITION lives HERE, unchanged, because /tmux:elaborate references it BY NAME ("authored ONCE in task-plan-generate.xml's <rule-to-goal-injection> block") — this block is the single source of truth for the procedure; deleting it would break the elaborator. Treat the text below as the shared spec elaborate binds to, not a step this generator runs.
CODE-RULE → GOAL INJECTION — the shared `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure. The <resolve-code-rules> block above loads the catalogue; THIS block turns each matched code rule into a goal's DAEMON-VISIBLE structured fields. Define the mechanic ONCE here; the Tier-2 elaborator (/tmux:elaborate) binds its own `--files`/`--phase` and invokes `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` immediately before composing a goal's concrete params. Agents COPY pre-rendered payload fields VERBATIM — never re-glob, re-derive severity, or re-render a command. Go owns ALL glob/severity/signal routing (the determinism boundary, internal/rules/coderules.go); nothing here re-matches or re-renders.
RUN (Bash, once per goal, immediately before composing that goal's goal-create params): `tmux-cli rules match --files <comma-joined deliverable paths of THIS goal> --phase <THIS goal's phase token> --json` It prints ONE JSON object (NOT a bare array): {"rules":[ {"id","severity","validate_kind","phase","paths"(array),"acceptance_line","validate_cmd"} … ],"warnings":[…]}. Every field is PRE-RENDERED by Go: • `acceptance_line` = `CR-<id>: <body>` for a `must` rule that has acceptance, else "" (empty string for `should` / empty-acceptance rules). • `validate_cmd` = a fail-closed negated grep `sh -c '! grep -rE "<signal>" <matched files>'` when the rule has a RUNNABLE signal, else "" (empty — a non-runnable signal was already baseline-dropped by Go; an empty `validate_cmd` means INJECT NOTHING for that signal, NEVER re-render it). • `validate_kind` ∈ {`automated`,`review`,`mixed`}; `paths` = the source code-rule file path(s).
[CODE-RULE-INJECTION] — apply these copy rules to the parsed object, then compose the goal-create call: 0. EMPTY GUARD: if `rules` is empty, inject NOTHING — no goal.md rules line, no acceptance, no validate, no investigator. (`warnings` is informational: print it, never inject it.) Skip steps 1–5. 1. goal.md rules line: add EXACTLY ONE line `Code rules: <comma-joined rules[].id> — read <comma-joined unique rules[].paths>` to the goal's rules/context. It SUPERSEDES the prose "read and honor <paths>" pointer from <resolve-code-rules> — same pointer, now carrying IDs. This line is a human/implementer POINTER, NOT enforcement; the structured fields (steps 2–4) are the enforcement ([[vacuous-gate-fix-four-surfaces]]). 2. ACCEPTANCE (must rules): for every rule whose `acceptance_line` is NON-EMPTY, COPY `acceptance_line` VERBATIM as one additional entry in the goal-create `acceptance[]` array (it is already `CR-<id>: <body>`). A `should` rule has an empty `acceptance_line`, so copying nothing is automatic (acceptance-bloat cap holds). One CR-<id> per must rule. 3. VALIDATE (hard gate — automated/mixed): for every rule whose `validate_cmd` is NON-EMPTY AND whose `validate_kind` is `automated` OR `mixed`, COPY `validate_cmd` VERBATIM as one additional `validate[]` entry, and mark that rule id ENFORCED. NEVER inject a rule whose `validate_cmd` is "" (non-runnable / baseline-dropped — Go already removed it). 4. CONVENTION-AUDIT (review): collect REVIEW_CMDS = the `validate_cmd` of every rule whose `validate_kind` is `review` AND whose `validate_cmd` is NON-EMPTY (these were NOT ENFORCED in step 3 ⇒ a given rule's grep lands in EITHER `validate[]` OR the investigator, NEVER both — no double enforcement). If REVIEW_CMDS is EMPTY ⇒ emit NO investigator (review rules ride the goal.md line + acceptance(if must) + spec citation; a command-less investigator is REJECTED by validateInvestigators). If NON-EMPTY ⇒ exactly ONE `convention-audit` investigator carries ALL of REVIEW_CMDS (never one-per-rule): • MERGE-OR-ADD: if this shard's base investigator set ALREADY emits a `convention-audit` investigator, MERGE — append REVIEW_CMDS to its Commands and extend its Fail with "any cited must code-rule violated; should violations reported as findings". Count unchanged. Otherwise ADD {Name:"Code-rule convention audit", Type:"convention-audit", Paths:<this goal's scope>, Commands:REVIEW_CMDS, Pass:"every command exits 0 — no forbidden pattern in the cited footprint", Fail:"any cited must code-rule violated; should violations reported as findings"}. 5. 2–4 CAP (AUTHORING-TIME — the daemon does NOT cap an explicit investigation_config; goal-create HARD-REJECTS >4 via validateInvestigators, and capInvestigators only runs on the daemon's derive-from-scratch path, never on your explicit list): after a MERGE the count is unchanged (always legal). After an ADD: if the base count is ≤3 the new count is ≤4 (legal). If the base count is already 4, you must FREE one slot — and ONLY when a `must` review rule applies (some rule with `validate_kind`=`review` has a NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line`); otherwise do NOT add the investigator (those review rules ride the goal.md line + spec citation, no drop). To free a slot, DROP the base investigator with the LOWEST keep-priority per investigatorTypePriority — drop ORDER: `static-analysis` first, then `architecture-check`, then `quality-gate`, then `test-execution`; NEVER drop `emission-check`/`own-suite-green`, and NEVER drop below 2 investigators total. Final `investigation_config` length MUST be within [2,4].
Inject ONLY into the STRUCTURED goal-create params (`acceptance` / `validate` / `investigation_config`); the goal.md rules line is a pointer, never the enforcement surface. The literal `tmux-cli rules match --files <deliverables> --phase <phase> --json` is invoked per goal — do NOT hand-build any RULE_INDEX.
conventions.fallback: If `tmux-cli rules resolve` fails or .tmux-cli/rules/ is missing, ABORT generation with the diagnostic "rules catalogue missing — run `tmux-cli project init` (or re-run setup) to materialize .tmux-cli/rules/, then re-run". NEVER generate goals without the loaded conventions. Conservative-inclusion warnings on stderr are NOT failures — proceed with the extra packs loaded.

step.title: Validate discovery outputs
step.rule: This step is a blocking gate. If any validation error is found, abort with a numbered error list and do NOT proceed to Step 1.

substep.title: File existence check
substep.action: Verify the following files and directories exist on disk using test -f / test -d:

For context-map.md: count the number of level-3 headings (### lines) under "Bounded Context Inventory" in bounded-contexts.md. If exactly one BC exists, skip the context-map.md check entirely (D-27). If two or more BCs exist, context-map.md is required.
For each missing required file, collect an error: "Missing required file: docs/architecture/<filename> — run /tmux:task-plan-discover first"
substep.rule: Do not stop at the first missing file. Check all files and collect all errors.

substep.title: Parse discovery files
substep.action: Read each discovery file and extract structured data:

parse.extract: Find all level-3 headings (### lines) under the "## Bounded Context Inventory" section. Each heading text is a BC name. Store as BC_LIST.
For each BC heading, find the "- **Aggregates:** " field. Parse the comma-separated values after the colon as aggregate names (trim whitespace). Store as AGGREGATE_MAP[bc_name] = [aggregate_names].

Find level-2 sections (## headings) — these are per-BC domain model files or sections. Under each, find level-3 headings (### lines) — these are aggregate names. Store for cross-checking with AGGREGATE_MAP.
For each aggregate, find "- Read model interface (query): " field. Store whether the value after the colon is empty or non-empty.
Find all "| Bounded Context | " table rows. The value in the second column is a BC name. Collect unique BC names referenced by endpoints. Store as ENDPOINT_MAP keys.

Find endpoints where Method is GET and Path has no trailing ID segment (e.g. /api/bookings vs /api/bookings/{id}), or where Description contains keywords "list", "search", or "index". These are collection/list actions. Tag each with its BC name.

Find and check non-emptiness of: base URL (line containing "Base URL" or "base_url"), test credentials (line containing "Test Credentials" or "test_credentials"), DB name (line containing "Test Database" or "DB name" or "db_name"), fixture command (line containing "Fixture Command" or "fixture_command"), Playwright status (line containing "Playwright" and "Status" or "playwright_status").

substep.title: Cross-file consistency checks
substep.action: Apply these 5 validation rules. Collect all errors — do not stop at the first failure.
substep.rule: Every BC name found in api-endpoints.md (from "Bounded Context" table cells) must have a matching ### heading in bounded-contexts.md under "Bounded Context Inventory". For each BC in api-endpoints.md that has no matching heading in bounded-contexts.md, collect error: "BC '<name>' found in api-endpoints.md but missing from bounded-contexts.md"

Every BC listed in bounded-contexts.md must have at least one aggregate in domain-model.md (at least one ### heading under that BC's section). For each BC with zero aggregates, collect error: "BC '<name>' has no aggregates in domain-model.md — every BC must have at least one"

Every BC that has GET endpoints with collection paths (list/search actions) in api-endpoints.md must have a non-empty "Read model interface (query):" field on at least one aggregate in domain-model.md. For each BC with list actions but no read model interface, collect error: "BC '<name>' has list/search actions but no read model interface noted in domain-model.md"

Every aggregate name listed in bounded-contexts.md under "- **Aggregates:** " must match a ### heading in domain-model.md for that BC. Comparison is case-insensitive and whitespace-trimmed. For each mismatch, collect error: "Aggregate name mismatch for BC '<bc_name>': bounded-contexts.md has '<name_a>', domain-model.md has '<name_b>'"

test-environment.md must exist and contain non-empty values for all of: base URL, test credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status. For each missing or empty field, collect error: "test-environment.md missing required field: <field_name>"

substep.title: test-environment.md field validation
substep.action: This substep performs detailed field-by-field validation of test-environment.md. For each of the 5 required fields (base URL, test credentials, DB name, fixture command, Playwright status), verify:

checks.check: Value after the field label is not empty, not just whitespace, and looks like a URL (contains :// or starts with http/https).
Value after the field label is not empty — at minimum a username or token is specified.
Value after the field label is not empty and not just whitespace.
Value after the field label is not empty — a concrete command string is present.
Value after the field label is not empty and contains one of: "installed", "not installed", "not applicable", "needs", or equivalent status indicator.

substep.action: For each field that fails validation, collect error: "test-environment.md missing required field: <field_name>"

branch.action: Print all collected errors as a numbered list with file references:
ABORT. Do NOT proceed to Step 1.
Print: "Discovery output validation passed — {{bc_count}} bounded contexts, {{aggregate_count}} aggregates, {{endpoint_bc_count}} BCs with endpoints verified."
Store BC_LIST, AGGREGATE_MAP, and ENDPOINT_MAP for use in subsequent steps.

step.title: First-run Preconditions backfill (idempotent)
step.rule: Runs once per plan after Step 0 passes and BEFORE any goal generation. It brings PRE-EXISTING goal.md files (authored by an earlier plan that predates the Preconditions schema) up to the current schema so the taskvisor §5 auto-resume loop can re-evaluate env/infra-blocked goals.
Idempotent: a goal.md that already contains a "## Preconditions" heading is left UNTOUCHED — never write a duplicate section. A fresh project with no existing goal.md files is a no-op.
step.action: Enumerate existing .tmux-cli/goals/goal-*/goal.md files. For each file that does NOT already contain a "## Preconditions" heading, perform substeps 0b.1–0b.2.

substep.title: Derive the goal's preconditions
substep.action: FIRST, derive the DB signals locally. Step 0b runs BEFORE substep 1.2, so HAS_DATABASE/RUN_TARGET/SERVICES_EXTERNAL are NOT yet in scope — compute them HERE, do NOT forward-reference 1.2. Read docs/architecture/test-environment.md and apply the IDENTICAL rules as substep 1.2: RUN_TARGET=docker if "Run Target"==docker else local; SERVICES_EXTERNAL=true if "DB/services location"==external (default false in docker mode); HAS_DATABASE=true if it defines DB connection fields (host, port, user, password, db_name). If test-environment.md is absent, treat HAS_DATABASE=false.

Parse env/service gates for the goal from the environment-gate template (.tmux-cli/templates/{{LANG}}-{{FRAMEWORK}}/environment-gate.md, falling back to _base) and the project AGENTS.md runtime/preconditions section.
Map each gate to a line: "- [env] VAR_NAME — remedy" (required env var) or "- [service] host:port — remedy" (reachable TCP service).

DB sub-branch — applies to the Gate-0 / environment-gate goal ONLY (goal-001, phase=gate — the goal whose gates the environment-gate template defines), never to any other goal. If HAS_DATABASE AND (RUN_TARGET=local OR SERVICES_EXTERNAL), ALSO append to that goal's derived list the two lines below, verbatim and byte-identical to WS1's fresh Gate-0 emission (substep 1.65):

lines.line: - [service] <host>:<port> — start/provision the test DB per docs/architecture/test-environment.md
- [env] DB_PASSWORD — set DB_PASSWORD from docs/architecture/test-environment.md (do not inline the value)

substep.rule: <host>/<port> MUST be substituted with the literal values from test-environment.md (e.g. 127.0.0.1 and 5432). NEVER emit shell-variable syntax ($DB_HOST/$DB_PORT) and NEVER inline the password — the [env] spec is the bare variable NAME (DB_PASSWORD), which the daemon checks via os.LookupEnv (GM-05). The daemon TCP-dials the literal [service] spec, so shell-variable syntax would never resolve.
substep.action: In docker/compose-internal mode (RUN_TARGET=docker AND NOT SERVICES_EXTERNAL), add NEITHER line — the compose-internal DB is provisioned by Scaffold (goal-002), so no Gate-0 ops precondition is required.
If no gate applies to this goal, use the single literal line "none".

substep.title: Inject the section, preserving all other content
substep.action: Insert a "## Preconditions" section (the derived list, or "none") into goal.md immediately BEFORE the "## Not In Scope" heading; if that heading is absent, append the section at end of file.
substep.rule: PRESERVE every other section verbatim — Description, Dependencies, Deliverables, Acceptance Criteria, Validation, Context, Investigation Config, Not In Scope. Only the new "## Preconditions" section is added.

step.title: Resolve runtime command-execution model (RUN_TARGET-aware)
step.rule: Runs after Step 0b and BEFORE any goal generation. Records HOW commands will be executed so the rest of generation emits the RIGHT commands — but does NOT hand-prefix them. The taskvisor daemon OWNS command-runtime wrapping: it resolves the project ExecRuntime from test-environment.md and wraps every validate/investigator command into the correct container at dispatch (internal/taskvisor wrapcmd.go / execruntime.go). So generation emits BARE, host-style commands and the daemon makes them runnable. This is the single seam that makes a docker-mode plan executable on a host with no local PHP — and there is exactly ONE source of truth (the daemon), never hand-maintained prefixes.
step.action: Set RUN_TARGET: read docs/architecture/test-environment.md "Run Target" — docker → RUN_TARGET=docker, else local. (IDENTICAL rule to step 0b / substep 1.2 / step 3.26 — do not drift.)

Fix the runtime SERVICE NAMES that step 2 task-R must create and the daemon execs into (they MUST agree, since the daemon defaults to these): APP_SVC = the PHP/app compose service, default "app" (prefer a service literally named "app"/"php" in the Published Ports block). NODE_SVC = a dedicated Node/Playwright service, default "e2e", created ONLY when RUN_TARGET=docker AND Playwright is applicable.

step.title: Generate Gate 0 goal (goal-001)
step.load: READ step-1-gate0.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Scaffold goal (goal-002)
step.load: READ step-2-scaffold.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate domain layer goals (one per BC)
step.load: READ step-3.14-domain.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Per-BC application layer goals
step.load: READ step-3.15-application.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate infrastructure goals
step.load: READ step-3.16-infrastructure.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Auth Bootstrap goal (conditional, phase=auth-bootstrap)
step.load: CONDITIONAL (N_auth_flows >= 1): if the condition holds, READ step-3.16a-auth-bootstrap.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate Fixtures goal (single goal, phase=fixtures)
step.load: READ step-3.17-fixtures.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Controller Path Resolver (shared)
step.load: READ step-3.17.0-controller-path-resolver.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Controller Action Goals
step.load: READ step-3.18-controller-actions.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Auth flow goals (phase=auth)
step.load: READ step-3.19-auth-flows.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate dev-env seed-admin goal (conditional, phase=seed)
step.load: CONDITIONAL (seed_default_admin=yes AND N_auth_flows >= 1): if the condition holds, READ step-3.19a-seed-admin.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate event listener goals (phase=event_listener)
step.load: READ step-3.20-event-listeners.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Error Handling goal (single goal, phase=error_handling)
step.load: READ step-3.21-error-handling.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate i18n translator-foundation goal (single goal, phase=cross-cutting)
step.load: READ step-3.21b-i18n-foundation.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate HTTP Middleware goal (conditional, phase=middleware)
step.load: CONDITIONAL (middleware selected in cross-cutting.md): if the condition holds, READ step-3.22-middleware.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate API documentation goal (phase=api_docs) — CONDITIONAL
step.load: CONDITIONAL (OpenAPI/nelmio selected in cross-cutting.md): if the condition holds, READ step-3.23-api-docs.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate Messenger transport goal (conditional, phase=messenger)
step.load: CONDITIONAL (async/Messenger selected in cross-cutting.md): if the condition holds, READ step-3.24-messenger.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate Health Check goal (single goal, phase=health_check)
step.load: READ step-3.25-health-check.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Docker/deployment goal (conditional, phase=docker)
step.load: CONDITIONAL (Docker/container/deployment ADR exists): if the condition holds, READ step-3.26-docker.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate CI/CD pipeline goal (conditional, phase=cicd)
step.load: CONDITIONAL (CI/CD platform selected in cross-cutting.md): if the condition holds, READ step-3.27-cicd.xml NOW and execute its content as this step; if not, skip without reading.

step.title: Generate Makefile / DX goal (single goal, phase=dx)
step.load: READ step-3.28-dx.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

step.title: Generate Final Gate goals (goal-T+3..T+5)
step.load: READ step-3.29-final-gates.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

validation-as-goal.intent: The daemon runs a goal's HEAVY validate stack (phpunit integration + deptrac + phpstan L9 + kernel boot) decoupled from implementation: per the validation-as-goal model, the heavy checks live in a DEDICATED validation goal, never inline in the implementer's supervising cycle. A red validation goal is terminal-to-itself (the daemon's CascadeFailure short-circuits on any goal whose `validates:` is set), so it never cascade-blocks the implementer it validates nor any unrelated downstream impl goal. Step 3.30 emits these validation goals; this token (`validation-as-goal`) marks the policy as active.
validation-as-goal.rule: Every implementation goal keeps a LIGHT presence validate (build + file-presence greps) so the every-goal-has-≥1-validate mandate (RC-A) holds; the heavy checks move to its phase's validation goal.
A validation goal carries `validates:` = the primary impl goal id it gates, an elevated `priority` (above the default 0), and `depends_on` the implementer(s) whose work it validates.

step.title: Generate per-phase validation goals (validation-as-goal)
step.load: READ step-3.30-validation-goals.xml NOW and execute its content as this step.

substep.title: Load generated goals
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Extract ordered list of all goals with their: goal ID, phase, depends_on array, BC assignment (if any).
Build GOAL_SEQUENCE — an ordered array of {id, phase, depends_on, bc} objects in goals.yaml file order.

substep.title: Validate strict phase ordering (G-04)
substep.action: Define PHASE_ORDER: gate=0, scaffold=1, domain=2, application=3, infrastructure=4, fixtures=5, error_handling=6, auth=7, action=8, event_listener=9. Cross-cutting phases (cross-cutting, middleware, api_docs, messenger, health_check, docker, cicd, dx) share rank=10 and are interchangeable among themselves. final_gate=11.
Walk GOAL_SEQUENCE. For each goal, verify its phase rank is >= the previous goal's phase rank (equal rank is allowed for same-phase goals and cross-cutting phases).

check.condition: any goal has phase rank LOWER than the preceding goal (excluding cross-cutting interchangeability)
check.action: Print error: "G-04 VIOLATION: {goal_id} (phase={phase}, rank={rank}) appears after {prev_goal_id} (phase={prev_phase}, rank={prev_rank}) — phase ordering broken."

substep.title: Validate depends_on acyclicity (G-15)
substep.action: For each goal in GOAL_SEQUENCE, verify that every ID in its depends_on array refers to a goal with a LOWER sequence index.
Build adjacency list from depends_on. Run cycle detection (DFS with visited/in-stack markers).

check.condition: any goal depends on a goal with higher or equal sequence index, OR cycle detected
check.action: Print error: "G-15 VIOLATION: circular dependency detected — {goal_id} depends on {dep_id} which appears at or after it in the sequence."

substep.title: Validate per-BC ordering (G-04)
substep.action: Group goals by BC assignment. For each BC, collect goals with phases domain, application, infrastructure.
Verify within each BC: domain goal appears before application goal, application goal appears before infrastructure goal.

check.condition: any BC has application before domain, or infrastructure before application
check.action: Print error: "G-04 VIOLATION: BC '{bc_name}' has {phase_a} goal ({goal_id_a}) before {phase_b} goal ({goal_id_b}) — must be domain → application → infrastructure."

substep.title: Validate controller actions after fixtures (G-04b)
substep.action: Find the fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) sequence index. Find all action goals (phase=action) sequence indices.
Verify every action goal has a sequence index higher than the fixtures goal.
Additionally verify every action goal's depends_on includes the fixtures goal ID.

check.condition: any action goal appears before fixtures or does not depend on fixtures
check.action: Print error: "G-04b VIOLATION: action goal {goal_id} (index={idx}) appears before or does not depend on fixtures goal {fixtures_id} (index={fixtures_idx})."

substep.title: Report success
substep.action: Print: "Ordering verification passed: all {N} goals satisfy G-04 (phase ordering), G-04b (actions after fixtures), G-15 (acyclic dependencies)."

step.title: Escalation bounce — turn an escalation need into a sequenced prerequisite (conditional, phase=generation)
step.objective: When generation is re-dispatched for a SINGLE bounced goal carrying an executor escalation (a missing out-of-scope prerequisite), create that prerequisite as a real early-phase goal and wire the bounced goal to wait on it. This is the runtime backstop for the rare prerequisite that the initial plan (Steps 0–4) did not anticipate — NOT a path the full initial plan ever takes.

substep.title: Detect the bounce mode and gate
substep.action: This step runs ONLY when generation was invoked for a single bounced goal in phase=generation (set by the supervisor's bounceToGeneration after an [EXECUTE:ESCALATE need=…] was routed as a spec-defect). Otherwise — a full initial plan from docs/architecture/* — SKIP this entire step.
Bind BOUNCED_GOAL_ID = the bounced goal's ID (the goal the supervisor re-dispatched generation for).

check.action: Print: "Not an escalation bounce — skipping Step 5."

substep.title: Read the escalation need from the newest correction
substep.action: List .tmux-cli/goals/<BOUNCED_GOAL_ID>/corrections/cycle-*.md and pick the NEWEST by cycle number (highest N).
Read it and extract the escalation `need` line (the concrete missing prerequisite the executor escalated). Bind NEED = that text.

check.condition: no corrections/cycle-*.md exists OR no need line found
check.action: Print diagnostic: "Escalation bounce for <BOUNCED_GOAL_ID> but no escalation need found in corrections/cycle-*.md — cannot synthesize a prerequisite." and STOP (do NOT loop; let the SpecRetries drain / human halt take over).

substep.title: Resolve the scaffold anchor
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=scaffold — bind SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR = its ID. The prerequisite depends ONLY on the scaffold anchor (the repo skeleton), NEVER on BOUNCED_GOAL_ID (that would create a cycle).

substep.title: Derive the prerequisite config from the need
substep.action: Derive PREREQ_DESCRIPTION (≤120 chars; MaxGoalDescriptionLength invariant), PREREQ_PHASE, and PREREQ_DELIVERABLE_AREA (the coarse deliverable path the prerequisite produces) from NEED. Per roadmap-only emission, do NOT derive validate or investigation_config here — the prerequisite is emitted as a skeleton and /tmux:elaborate authors its concrete fields at dispatch against the real tree (design §5).
substep.rule: PREREQ_PHASE: if NEED concerns authentication / JWT / identity / security config / User entity → phase=auth-bootstrap (reuse the 3.16a auth-bootstrap shape: security.yaml + User entity + JWT keypair as applicable). Otherwise → an early gate/infra prerequisite: phase=gate for a build/config/tooling prerequisite, or phase=infrastructure when the need is a real infra deliverable (an "infra-bootstrap").
The prerequisite is a STANDALONE early-phase goal — it must not re-declare anything the bounced goal owns; it provides only what NEED says is missing.

substep.title: Create the prerequisite goal via goal-create (roadmap skeleton)
substep.action: Call goal-create MCP tool with a roadmap SKELETON (no validate/investigation_config — those are authored by /tmux:elaborate at dispatch):
substep.rule: depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR] ONLY — NEVER [BOUNCED_GOAL_ID] (a cycle; goal-add-prerequisite would reject it anyway).
substep.action: Bind NEW_PREREQ_ID = the returned goal ID.

check.action: Print the error with full context and STOP.

substep.title: Wire the bounced goal to the new prerequisite via goal-add-prerequisite
substep.action: Call goal-add-prerequisite MCP tool with goal_id=BOUNCED_GOAL_ID, prerequisite_id=NEW_PREREQ_ID. This appends NEW_PREREQ_ID to the bounced goal's depends_on (append order is harmless — NextPendingGoal gates on DependsOnSatisfied, so the daemon runs NEW_PREREQ_ID first) and increments the bounced goal's escalation_count.

check.action: Print diagnostic: "Escalation cap reached for <BOUNCED_GOAL_ID> — refusing to chain another prerequisite. Halting; let SpecRetries drain / a human intervene." and STOP. Do NOT loop or retry.
Print: "Escalation resolved: created <NEW_PREREQ_ID> (phase=<PREREQ_PHASE>) and wired <BOUNCED_GOAL_ID> to depend on it."

step.title: Handoff to planning
step.action: Print: "Generation complete. Run /tmux:plan — its blind audit gate (step 11a) audits the finalized plan via a serial native sub-agent before handing off to /tmux:supervisor. With require_plan_approval enabled, execution stays blocked until plan-approval.md records ≥90% with zero open SEV-1/SEV-2."

execution-rules.rule: goal-create MCP: single call per goal emitting a roadmap SKELETON — never batch; never author concrete fields
All discovery data comes from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16) — never from conversation context or memory
Discovery output files are read-only — never modify them during generation

Template resolution uses two-tier lookup: overlay directory first, then _base fallback — never hardcode template content. Templates inform the elaborator (Tier 2), not skeleton emission; the roadmap generator reads conventions only to derive the coarse deliverable_area topology.
Validate ALL consistency rules before reporting errors — collect every error, never stop at the first
Goal descriptions max 120 chars — detailed acceptance/validate criteria are authored later by /tmux:elaborate, never inlined into the skeleton's description
depends_on IDs dynamically read from goals.yaml after each goal-create — never hardcode goal IDs

ROADMAP SKELETON on EVERY enumerated goal-create (two-tier, design §5): every goal-creation call from Step 3.14 onward (MCP goal-create or `taskvisor goal add`) passes status=roadmap + description + phase + depends_on + a coarse deliverable_area, and OMITS validate/acceptance/scope/goal.md/investigation_config. The legacy ">=1 validate + >=1 acceptance on every goal" mandate does NOT apply to roadmap skeletons — /tmux:elaborate authors those structured fields at dispatch against the real tree (the goal-create capability accepts a status=roadmap skeleton with no validate without rejecting it). NEVER pass prose-only or predicted validate/acceptance for a skeleton. EXCEPTION: the two bootstrap goals — goal-001 (Gate 0) and goal-002 (Scaffold) — remain fully-specced born-pending (the bootstrap carve-out; see the <llm> ROADMAP-ONLY mandate), since they precede any tree for elaboration to read.

deliverable_area on EVERY goal-create: pass a single NON-EMPTY COARSE deliverable path in the resolved topology (e.g. `contexts/{BC}/src/Application/` or `projects/{app}/src/Http/ErrorHandling/`) — NOT a glob array, NOT a per-file path. depinfer keys produce/consume overlap on deliverable_area; the elaborator refines it into precise `scope` globs at dispatch. Because every roadmap skeleton is later elaborated and /tmux:elaborate REQUIRES a non-empty deliverable_area to seed its tree read, NEVER omit it — a repo-wide goal (final gate, cross-cluster validation goal) uses a COARSE repo-root or main-source-root anchor (e.g. `./` or `contexts/`), not an empty value. Omission is reserved for legacy hand-authored fully-specced pending goals, never roadmap skeletons.
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` — compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.

llm.critical-rules: * ROADMAP-ONLY REFRAME (two-tier, design §5/§6): from Step 3.14 onward this generator emits SKELETONS (status=roadmap + description + phase + depends_on + coarse deliverable_area) and nothing concrete. (Bootstrap carve-out: goal-001 Gate 0 / Step 1 and goal-002 Scaffold / Step 2 stay fully-specced born-pending — they precede any tree for elaboration to read.) The per-shard descriptions BELOW that name acceptance-criteria catalogs (EH-01..EH-08, PD-01..PD-20, AU-01..AU-10, …), validate commands, investigator counts, scope globs, and [CODE-RULE-INJECTION] describe what /tmux:elaborate AUTHORS AT DISPATCH against the real tree for the 3.14–3.30 goals — they are NOT emitted by this skill (except the Step 1/2 bootstrap bullets, which this skill still emits). Read every such line as "the elaborator authors this for the goal this shard enumerated." What THIS skill still owns from those descriptions: which goals exist per phase (enumeration), each goal's phase, its depends_on wiring, and its coarse deliverable_area. The DAG analyses (Step 4 / G-04, depinfer, plan-audit) and Step 0 stay Tier-1 and operate on skeletons. * Step 0 is a blocking validation gate — never generate goals if validation fails * All discovery data comes from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context * Discovery output files are read-only — never modify them during generation * Collect ALL validation errors before reporting — never stop at first error * Single-BC projects skip context-map.md check (D-27) * Step 1 (Gate 0) creates goal-001 with phase=gate — 2-4 investigators, max_retries=5, no dev-deps * Gate 0 checks come from environment-gate.md templates (overlay then base), never hardcoded (E-10) * DB credentials referenced via docs/architecture/test-environment.md only — never inlined (GM-05) * Step 2 (Scaffold) creates goal-002 with phase=scaffold, depends_on=[goal-001] — 4 investigators, 4 fan-out tasks (task-3 conditional on HAS_DATABASE; + task-R when RUN_TARGET=docker), max_retries=3 * Scaffold reads from templates (php-symfony overlay → _base fallback) and docs/architecture/* — never hardcoded (G-16) * Deptrac {BC}DomainAndShare composite layer uses previo2 bool collector pattern from php-symfony/bounded-contexts.md * Step 3.14 (Domain) creates one goal per BC with phase=domain, depends_on=[goal-002], 3-4 investigators, fan-out by aggregate count (>1 → multi-task with task-0 shared + task-1..N per aggregate), max_retries=3 * Domain goals: PD-01..PD-20 acceptance criteria, private constructors (PD-01), named factories (PD-02), final VOs with equals() (PD-03..PD-05), past-tense events (PD-06), collect-only DomainEventTrait (PD-17) * Zero Infrastructure/Application imports in Domain namespace (PD-09), no Doctrine annotations (PD-13), cross-BC refs via ID VOs only (PD-15) * ACL interfaces in consuming BC's contexts/{BC}/src/Domain/ACL/ (PD-16), shared types in contexts/previo/src/ not duplicated (PD-18, PD-19) * Share type ownership: first BC processed claims DomainEventTrait + base DomainException; subsequent BCs reference but don't regenerate * File-disjoint rule enforced on multi-task fan-out — shared files (DomainEventTrait, base exception, Share types) go into task-0 prerequisite * Step 3.15 (Application) creates one goal per BC with phase=application, depends_on=own BC's domain goal ID (dynamically read from goals.yaml), 3 investigators, single-task fan-out (multi-task only if >6 handlers), max_retries=3 * Application goals: PA-01..PA-12 acceptance criteria, one handler per use case (PA-01), port-only injection (PA-02), zero infra imports (PA-03/PA-04), command returns void/scalar (PA-06), query returns DTO (PA-07) * Application layer does NOT dispatch domain events — flush-then-dispatch is infrastructure's job * Read model interfaces live in Application/ReadModel/, not Domain (PD-08) * Step 3.16 (Infrastructure) creates one goal per BC with phase=infrastructure, depends_on=own BC's application goal ID, 3-4 investigators, fan-out by entity count (>3 → multi-task), max_retries=3 * Infrastructure goals: no annotations on domain objects (PI-03), flush-then-dispatch in write repos (PI-06), real DB in integration tests (PI-11), migrations generated not hand-written (PI-10) * ACL adapters (PI-16) and custom DBAL types (PI-08) are conditional — included only when cross-BC deps or compound VOs exist * Step 3.16a (Auth Bootstrap) is CONDITIONAL — emitted ONLY when N_auth_flows >= 1 (skip entirely on 0). Single goal, phase=auth-bootstrap, depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_GOAL_ID], 3 investigators, max_retries=3. OWNS config/packages/security.yaml (firewall/provider/authenticator) + User entity with password hashing + (when uses_jwt) lexik/jwt-authentication-bundle config + bin/console lexik:jwt:generate-keypair. Carries AU-08/AU-09 (relocated from per-flow auth goals) + AB-JWT (conditional on uses_jwt). User entity defaults to contexts/previo/src/Domain/Model/User.php if no identity BC exists. Stored as AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID for steps 3.18 (auth_required actions) and 3.19 (all auth flows). * Step 3.17 (Fixtures) creates a single goal with phase=fixtures, depends_on=ALL infra goal IDs (dynamically collected), 3 investigators, single-task fan-out, max_retries=3 * Fixtures depends_on MUST include EVERY infrastructure goal ID from goals.yaml — never just the last one (G-03) * Test credentials reference docs/architecture/test-environment.md only — never hardcoded in fixtures (GM-05, FX-02) * Step 3.18 (Controller Actions) creates one goal per non-auth action (G-05), phase=action, depends_on=[own BC infra + fixtures + error handling + all auth goals] + AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID (only for auth_required actions), 2-3 investigators, 5 deliverables (GM-12), max_retries=3 * Auth endpoints (## Auth Flows section) are EXCLUDED from controller action goals — handled by auth step 3.19 only * PC-01..PC-17 encoded in acceptance; PC-07..PC-14 conditional on Playwright availability * Fan-Out paths from api-endpoints.md used directly for deliverable file paths — never derived from conventions * Step 3.19 (Auth Flows) creates one goal per auth flow (N_auth_flows), phase=auth, depends_on=[fixtures + error handling + AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID], 3 investigators, 5 deliverables (security.yaml + User entity NOT declared — provided by auth-bootstrap), max_retries=3 * Auth flows read from docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md Step 6.2 output — one goal per flow (Register, Login, Logout, Token Refresh, Password Reset) * Auth goals carry AU-01..AU-10 (flow-specific) + PC-01..PC-17 (all flows) acceptance criteria * AU-08/AU-09 (security bundle, password hashing) are OWNED by the auth-bootstrap goal (3.16a), NOT per-flow auth goals; per-flow goals keep AU-07/AU-10 + flow-specific AU-01..AU-06 and depend on AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID. AU-05/AU-06 (role access) tested within Login/Register * Auth goal IDs stored as AUTH_GOAL_IDS — all subsequent action goals MUST depend on them * Never double-count auth endpoints in per-BC N_actions — auth counted only in N_auth_flows * Step 3.19a (Seed Default Admin) is CONDITIONAL — emitted ONLY when seed_default_admin=yes in test-environment.md `## Bootstrap / Seed Default Admin` AND N_auth_flows >= 1 (skip silently when not requested; skip + warn when 0 auth flows). Single goal, phase=seed, depends_on=[auth-bootstrap goal; FALLBACK first phase=auth/Register goal], max_retries=2, acceptance SA-01..SA-06, 2 investigators (command-run + no-inlined-secret file-check). Validate runs `bin/console app:seed-admin --env=dev` (NEVER --env=test) and asserts the admin row exists. Credentials referenced by env-var NAME only (admin_identifier_env / admin_password_env) — never inlined (GM-05); never folded into the --env=test fixtures goal; never seeds prod. * Step 3.22 (HTTP Middleware) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no middleware selected. If middleware selected: single goal, phase=middleware, depends_on=[fixtures goal ID], 3 investigators, max_retries=3 * Middleware criteria MW-01 (CORS), MW-02 (logging), MW-03 (rate limiting) are each conditional on that feature being selected. MW-04 (kernel registration order) is always included. * Middleware files live in projects/{app}/src/Http/Middleware/ (cross-cutting deployable-app layer, not per-BC) * Step 3.20 (Event Listeners) creates one goal per event listener, phase=event_listener, depends_on=[own BC infra + fixtures] (cross-BC also depends on source BC infra), 2-3 investigators, max_retries=3 * Event listener goals use EV-01..EV-13 acceptance criteria: EV-01..EV-07 always, EV-08/EV-09 conditional on is_cross_bc, EV-10/EV-11/EV-13 always, EV-12 conditional on async transport * Event listeners: integration tests only — NO Playwright E2E tests (spec lines 329-332). Action E2E tests assert HTTP response + direct DB state, not event handler side effects. * Cross-BC listeners: Published Language DTO in contexts/previo/src/Event/, ACL adapter in consuming context's contexts/{BC}/src/Bundle/Application/Acl/, message processor/handler in the context app layer contexts/{BC}/app/src/Adapter/ — imports only its own context + the shared-kernel (contexts/previo) namespace * Intra-BC listeners: handler in contexts/{BC}/app/src/EventListener/, no ACL needed, event class from own context's Domain/Event/ * Event dispatch chain: aggregate collects via DomainEventTrait (PD-17) → infra repo dispatches after flush (PI-06) → Messenger routes to handler (EV-07) → handler executes side effect * Event listener goals NEVER duplicate criteria already covered by domain goals (PD-06/PD-17 event naming and collection) or infra goals (PI-06 flush-then-dispatch) — event listener goals test the handler/subscription side only * Step 3.21 (Error Handling) creates a single UNCONDITIONAL goal with phase=error_handling, depends_on=[fixtures goal ID], 3 investigators (implementation-check, quality-gate, test-execution), single-task fan-out, max_retries=3. Records ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID for steps 3.18/3.19. All 8 EH-01..EH-08 criteria are unconditional. Error handling files live in projects/{app}/src/Http/ErrorHandling/ (cross-cutting deployable-app layer, not per-BC). * Step 3.23 (API Documentation) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no OpenAPI/nelmio selected. If selected: single goal, phase=api_docs, depends_on=ALL action + auth goal IDs (dynamically resolved), AD-01..AD-04 criteria, 3 investigators, max_retries=3. AUTH_GOAL_IDS may be empty (valid); ACTION_GOAL_IDS must not be empty. * Step 3.24 (Messenger Transport) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no async/Messenger selection. If selected: single goal (cross-cutting, not per-BC), phase=messenger, depends_on=ALL event listener goal IDs (dynamically resolved from goals.yaml phase=event_listener), MT-01..MT-05 criteria, 2 investigators (config check + consumer test), max_retries=3. Transport DSN never hardcoded — references test-environment.md and cross-cutting.md ADR choice (GM-05). Validate commands use --limit=1 --time-limit=5 for CI-safe consumer exit. * Step 3.25 (Health Check) creates a single UNCONDITIONAL goal with phase=health_check, depends_on=ALL infrastructure goal IDs (dynamically collected from goals.yaml), HC-01..HC-03 criteria, 2 investigators (endpoint response check, degraded health check), single-task fan-out, max_retries=3. Services list derived from discovery outputs (cross-cutting.md, bounded-contexts.md) — never hardcoded. * Step 3.26 (Docker/Deployment) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if no ADR in docs/architecture/adrs/ mentions Docker/container/deployment. If selected: single goal, phase=docker, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs (full goals.yaml scan, not phase-filtered), DK-01..DK-05 criteria, 3 investigators (build check, compose services, app accessibility), max_retries=3. Description 70 chars. Reads services from ADRs + cross-cutting.md + test-environment.md. * Step 3.27 (CI/CD Pipeline) is CONDITIONAL — skip entirely if docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md has no CI/CD platform selected. If selected: single goal, phase=cicd, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs (full goals.yaml scan, not phase-filtered), CI-01..CI-04 criteria, 2 investigators (config validation, pipeline syntax), max_retries=3. CI_PLATFORM detected from cross-cutting.md (GitHub Actions / GitLab CI). CI_CONFIG_PATH derived from platform (.github/workflows/ci.yml or .gitlab-ci.yml). * Step 3.28 (Makefile/DX) creates a single UNCONDITIONAL goal with phase=dx, depends_on=ALL non-gate/scaffold goal IDs (dynamically collected from goals.yaml — exclude phase=gate and phase=scaffold), DX-01..DX-04 criteria, 3 investigators (Makefile targets, .env.test file check, lint/fix cycle), single-task fan-out, max_retries=3. .env.test variables reference docs/architecture/test-environment.md only — never hardcoded (GM-05). Not-in-scope excludes CI/CD pipeline config, Docker targets, and production Makefile targets. * Step 3.29 (Final Gates) creates exactly 3 UNCONDITIONAL goals with phase=final_gate, depends_on=ALL previous goal IDs (full goals.yaml scan), max_retries=5. Three goals for retry isolation: Deptrac Full (FG-01..FG-07, 2 investigators), E2E Regression (FG-08/FG-09/G-08, 2 investigators), Quality Sweep (FG-10..FG-18, 3 investigators). ALL_GOAL_IDS dynamically collected from goals.yaml — never hardcoded. G-08 compliance: last 3 goals in goals.yaml must be these final gates. FG-01..FG-18 all covered with no gaps across the 3 goals. * Investigation Config structure (GM-08/GM-09): Every goal.md Investigation Config section MUST have 2-4 investigators. Each investigator has: type (from Investigation Types Reference Table at spec lines 955-979), paths (file paths to check), commands (shell commands), pass/fail criteria. Conditional investigators include a condition field. Never hardcode investigator definitions — derive types from the Reference Table. * Investigation Config OBJECTIVITY (GM-09b) — convergence-critical: every investigator's pass/fail MUST be decidable from a command's exit status or output alone — an exit code, a numeric threshold (wc -l / grep -c / "must output N"), or a presence check (grep -rl returning ≥1 line). The words "coverage", "covers", "appropriate(ly)", "properly", "sufficient", "demonstrably", "as needed", "where applicable", "present" (as a coverage claim) are BANNED in pass/fail text — they make the verdict a judgment call, which is what stalls the dispatch→validate→correct loop (the validator flip-flops, the implementer gets prose, and the goal burns its code-retry budget re-flagging the same finding). A green test suite is NOT proof a required behavior is tested. When an acceptance criterion asserts that specific behaviors/invariants are TESTED, DECOMPOSE it (next rule); the test-execution investigator's pass is then "suite exits 0 AND non-empty AND every presence-grep returns ≥1", never "suite green with X coverage". * Acceptance DECOMPOSITION (GM-09c): never emit a compound "tests cover: A, B, C, ..." criterion. Emit one ATOMIC, individually-greppable criterion per item, and for any invariant/uniqueness/existence rule emit a pair — (a) enforcing code exists in src/ and (b) a test asserting the violation/throw exists in tests/. Mirror every decomposed item into BOTH the acceptance array AND a matching presence-grep in the Investigation Config + VALIDATE array, so a single missing item can never hide behind a green suite. * Investigation Config delivery (M3): pass investigators via the `investigation_config` goal-create param — NEVER also write a literal Investigation Config markdown heading into the `context` param or a goal.md template. goal-create renders exactly one Investigation Config section from the param (or the deriveInvestigators fallback when omitted); a duplicate produces a doubled or nested section that breaks investigate.xml parsing. * Test-environment reference (GM-05): Every goal.md involving database access, E2E testing, or service connectivity MUST include "See docs/architecture/test-environment.md" in its context section. Credentials, DSNs, and connection parameters are NEVER inlined — always reference test-environment.md. * Fan-out hints (G-17): Every goal.md MUST include a Fan-Out Hints section. Single-task goals state "single-task". Multi-task goals list task-0 (shared prerequisites), task-1..N (parallel work units), with explicit depends_on within the goal. Fan-out hints drive tasks.yaml generation — missing hints cause task planning failures. * Playwright conditional skip: When HAS_FRONTEND=false (from Step 1.2 line 141): skip PC-07..PC-14 in acceptance arrays (Step 3.18.3), skip E2E investigators in Investigation Config, skip E2E Final Gate goal (Step 3.29.3), and print a warning for each skip. HAS_FRONTEND=true preserves full behavior unchanged. * Ordering verification (Step 4): After all goals are generated, Step 4 validates phase ordering (G-04), depends_on acyclicity (G-15), per-BC ordering domain→application→infrastructure (G-04), and controller actions after fixtures (G-04b). Any violation is fatal — ABORT with diagnostic naming the violating goal ID and rule. * Step 5 (Escalation bounce) is CONDITIONAL — runs ONLY when generation is re-dispatched for a SINGLE bounced goal in phase=generation (after an [EXECUTE:ESCALATE need=…] was routed as a spec-defect); a full initial plan SKIPS it. Read the bounced goal's newest corrections/cycle-*.md, extract the escalation `need`, derive a prerequisite {description≤120, phase, validate, investigation_config} (auth/JWT/identity → phase=auth-bootstrap; else phase=gate/infrastructure), goal-create it with depends_on=[SCAFFOLD_ANCHOR] (NEVER the bounced goal — that is a cycle), then goal-add-prerequisite(goal_id=BOUNCED_GOAL_ID, prerequisite_id=NEW_PREREQ_ID). Append order is harmless (NextPendingGoal gates on DependsOnSatisfied, so the daemon runs the prerequisite first). On an escalation-cap error, print the diagnostic and STOP — never loop (let SpecRetries drain / a human halt take over). A worker must NEVER call goal-create/goal-add-prerequisite — these are generation-only (they race the daemon). * Subsequent tasks insert Steps 6+ above the closing comment in <flow>
